_source: Planetfall pp.137-138, Uncertain Terrain Features (Optional Rule)
_description: For more unpredictable battlefields. Mission briefings specify how many to place. 3 inch diameter markers. Cannot fire across until revealed. Revealed when crew within 9 inches, or within 18 inches and LoS, at end of each battle round. Roll D100 to determine actual terrain.

entries.name: Plant growths
entries.description: These plants do not inhibit movement, but figures within the feature count as being in cover.

entries.name: Heavy growth
entries.description: An Area terrain feature that provides cover. Example: Forest.

entries.name: Heavy growth (concerning)
entries.description: An Area terrain feature that provides cover. If the mission uses Contacts, place one marker in the center.

entries.name: Heavy growth (promising)
entries.description: An Area terrain feature that provides cover. If a squad member enters the feature, roll 1D6. On 6, roll once on the Post-Mission Finds table.

entries.name: Difficult going
entries.description: Difficult ground; does not affect visibility. Example: Swamp.

entries.name: Difficult going (concerning)
entries.description: Difficult ground that does not affect visibility. If the mission uses Contacts, place one marker in the center.

entries.name: Difficult going (promising)
entries.description: Difficult ground that does not affect visibility. If a squad member enters the feature, roll 1D6. On 6, roll once on the Post-Mission Finds table.

entries.name: Difficult going (dense)
entries.description: Area feature that provides cover and acts as Difficult ground. Example: Thick forest or jungle.

entries.name: Terrain block
entries.description: Block feature that cannot be entered but can be climbed, such as boulders.
